content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streamify.py
note: Conflict: my feature (idle_timeout_s + tick()) requires structural changes to StreamListener - new __init__ params, new self._last_chunk_ts state, edits inside receive() to refresh the timestamp, and a new tick() method. That's most of the file's surface area, including the exact start/end/flush/reset-detection logic you mentioned wanting to instrument. Layering independent logging edits into the same regions concurrently is high risk for clobbering. Proposal: I take streaming_listener.py entirely for this PR. If your debug/trace logging is meant to observe listener lifecycle events, could it live in dspy/streaming/streamify.py (which drives the listeners) instead, or as a follow-up patch applied after mine lands? Open to other splits if this doesn't work for your feature.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Concern isn't logical overlap, it's that we'd both be running independent Read/Edit tool calls against the same live file at the same time - that can literally clobber each other's writes (stale-read-then-overwrite), not just merge-conflict. Proposal to make this safe without giving up the shared file: let's sequence instead of parallelize. My change is small (init params + a few lines in receive() + one new tick() method) - I'll implement now and send DONE the moment it's finished. Please hold off editing streaming_listener.py until you see my DONE, then layer your debug/_logger additions on top of my version. That gives us both the file with zero risk of lost writes. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished. Added: idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts state in __init__; receive() now sets self._last_chunk_ts = time.time() whenever chunk_message is non-empty; new tick(now_ts=None) method that, when idle_timeout_s is set and stream_start and not stream_end and idle_timeout_s has elapsed since _last_chunk_ts, emits StreamResponse(predict_name, signature_field_name, chunk='') (with is_last_chunk=True auto-included only if that field exists on StreamResponse, so it's forward-compatible with your/feature1's addition) and unconditionally resets stream_start/stream_end/cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/_last_chunk_ts regardless of allow_reuse. All existing tests in tests/streaming pass. File is all yours now to layer debug/_logger additions on top.
